title_guess: Bath: Bath Marina, Bristol cycle path

pages.text:
Friday 4th May. 2001

5.15.  Simon, Charles, Eve + Tonic left with our 3 bikes (Simon's + Eve's new collapsible bikes) for Bath; we want to go on the Bath/Bristol cycle path. A bank holiday w/end, so lots of traffic.
                        __Bath Marina****__

Saturday 5th May
        Bright sunshine, but cold north wind. We set off on a bike ride. The campsite is sheltered, but it's quite cold riding. We have to put Tonic on a lead because he darts in front of cyclists. Simon trained him to run on the left. We cycled to Bitton railway station, where they have steam trains. It was 11 miles around trip. We had a picnic lunch back at the van.
        In the afternoon Simon + Charles + Tonic set off to Bath to see where to tie up bikes the next day. Eve read. Tonic came back too tired [strikethrough: out] to eat supper. (16 miles he'd run)

Eve bought a disposable bbque & Charles cooked bacon.
   __May 6th__
   Cold. Cold. Simon & Eve about to cycle off to church, but S. stayed behind with Charles because he had a puncture. Emmanuel church just along the road, very low, very freindly. The church warden, Mrs. Hune, in the M.V. Simon biked a bit with Tonic.
   Charles is biking & playing hide-&-seek with other boys on the campsite. He finally did so many skids that he caused an enormous bald patch hole in his tyre, so we can't ride bikes anymore.
   We caught a bus into Bath. We were incredibly lucky with bus times going & coming.
   We saw the William Herschel Museum in a small Georgian town house. He lived with his sister, Caroline, who was also an astronomer. From his garden, with a home-made telescope, he discovered Uranus.

He had originally come to England from Germany to play the organ in Bath.
   Then we ~~hada cop~~ saw the Micromuseum where there are tiny sculptures by Willard [illegible] Adams & Eve carved into a pencil point & the statue of Liberty in the eye of a needle, viewed through magnifying glasses.
   We had a cup of tea & looked at Bath Abbey. Nice embroidered Plaques show links to West Indies. Pale stone.
   We had a

captions: trencher supper at Sally Lunn on a Sally Lunn bun. The basement shows floor levels from Roman times. We had left Tonic in the van too, so we hurried back to let him out. Simon
